textDisplay: Solid build for the money. I paid $400 for mine at a woodworking store and yours looks just as usable. Nice work.
What kind of vise did you use? Looks beefy.
Yolanda Hardware 9 inch quick release. Got it on sale for $58. Does the job.
My daughter's quince is in October and I have been stressed for months. This timeline just took a huge weight off. Thank you for being so specific with the dates.
Fellow event planner here. The 9-month vendor lock is so important and nobody talks about it. Venues book fast in Vegas.
That marinade ratio is legit. Lime juice overnight is the move. Most recipes skip that step.
The fact that you showed the gaps instead of hiding them is why I subscribed. Real learning happens from mistakes.
Appreciate that. Not going to edit out a gap I can fit a nickel in.
8 dozen is rookie numbers in my house haha. But seriously the masa texture you got looks perfect. What brand of masa harina?
This is beautiful. So important to keep these traditions alive. My kids know exactly who is on our ofrenda and why.
I was quoted $18,000 for everything and almost fainted. This video broke it down and I realized I can cut $3,000 without cutting quality. Thank you.
Florals and the cake are the two places where you can get a lot back without guests noticing. DJ is not one of them. Bad DJ ruins a night.
Solid build for the money. Nice clean joinery.
This arch tutorial saved my daughter's party, thank you!
Your royal icing tips are the clearest I've found.
Half-finished garage projects is my entire life lol
Taller vases every time. Preaching to the choir here.
The rosemary from a cutting got me. Love that.
What blade did you use for that oak? Looks smooth.
Storing arch frames in a one-car garage is real content 😂
Made this pot roast, whole family cleaned their plates.
Subscribed. Real projects from a real shop.
Booking you next spring, your setups are clean.
Clearance hardwood is underrated. Great frame.
